red_baseline.reason: T009 froze the T010 bounded query, cursor, correlation, redaction, and zero-write adoption contract.
red_baseline.summary: The schema-bound test passes; remaining failures are missing T010 modules and T010-owned operation correlation behavior.

validation.summary: 11/11 bounded query, cursor, redaction, adoption, and correlation tests passed.
Nine capabilities/diagnosis/project/adoption/task/log/event operations produced valid normalized AgentOperationResult values with zero effects.
Resource identity is digest-bound and the cursor remains 384 characters, below the public 512-character schema limit.
Syntax check passed for 74 JavaScript files.
215 total; 214 passed; 1 existing POSIX platform skip; 0 failures; 129.5 seconds.

validation.command: query implementation mutation and whitespace scan
validation.summary: No filesystem/process/listener mutation primitive or trailing whitespace appears in T010 query implementation paths.

review.evidence: Query handlers only project injected existing authorities; observation cursors HMAC-bind kind/resource digest/query/high-water/expiry and stay within the public token limit; logs redact before byte accounting; malformed JSONL remains warning-only; adoption scans a trusted root without following symlinks or writing; correlation selects no work and exposes get/reconcile only for one candidate. Full regression passes.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Project/task/operation handlers project existing registry, run, journal, log, and event providers without creating a second state authority.
Capabilities expose exactly the approved registry and low-only mutation boundary; bounded handlers add no deferred or risky surface.
Correlation and recovery handlers never accept an original mutation handler; only a unique record can produce get/reconcile next actions.
Logs/events are resource-bound, cursor-paged, capped by accepted record/line/byte limits, and cannot accept arbitrary paths or follow mode.
Every project/task/adoption handler takes project identity/root from Kernel-resolved context, never public filesystem inputs.
Adoption uses the trusted registered project root and has no Plugin cwd or installation-path fallback.
adoption.inspect returns a bounded proposed plan/digest; adoption.apply remains absent from registry, capabilities, and next actions.
Adoption implementation contains no write/spawn/listen primitive and before/after project/state-home inventories are byte-identical.
Cursor integrity binds kind, resource digest, normalized query, high-water, offsets, issue/expiry times; redaction precedes response byte counting.
Unique correlation produces operation.get/reconcile for exactly the existing operation ID.
Zero and ambiguous correlations have no next actions, while high-water pagination cannot turn into permanent follow.
